title: veronica subsessilis var. subintegra
intro: Veronica subsessilis var. subintegra, also known as the small-flowered veronica, is a variety of flowering plant in the family Plantaginaceae. It is native to the mountainous regions of Asia and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and treating skin conditions such as eczema and wounds. In traditional herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of Veronica subsessilis var. subintegra are typically dried and made into teas, infusions, or applied topically as a salve or poultice.
